_meta.dna: devops(system) = ci(green) * cd(auto) * monitoring(real) * security(layered) * testing(complete) * infra(resilient)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor lists 7-10 seed questions. During execution, expand to 100 questions per persona by probing deeper into the domain. Search the actual codebase for evidence.

name: Tower of DevOps
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

questions: Is there a CI pipeline that runs on every push?
Do all tests pass in CI before merge is allowed?
Is the CI pipeline fast enough (< 10 minutes)?
Are CI failures visible and actionable?
Is the CI configuration version-controlled?
Are there flaky tests that intermittently fail in CI?
Is the test suite comprehensive enough to catch regressions?
Is deployment automated (git push → production)?
Is there a staging environment for pre-production testing?
Is the deployment pipeline documented?
Is there a rollback mechanism?
Is the deployment idempotent?
Are deployment secrets managed securely?
Is there blue-green or canary deployment capability?
Is the Docker image size minimized (multi-stage builds)?
Is the Dockerfile using appropriate base images?
Are unnecessary files excluded via .dockerignore?
Is the build cache used effectively?
Are health checks configured in the container?
Is the container running as non-root?
Is the image scanning for vulnerabilities?
Is Cloud Run configured with appropriate min/max instances?
Is cold start time acceptable (< 5 seconds)?
Is the CPU allocation correct (1 vCPU vs 2)?
Is memory allocation appropriate for the workload?
Is the concurrency setting optimized?
Is the request timeout configured correctly?
Are environment variables managed securely (Secret Manager)?
Is there application-level performance monitoring?
Are there metrics for response time (p50, p95, p99)?
Is CPU and memory utilization tracked?
Are there alerts for performance degradation?
Is there distributed tracing for cross-service requests?
Are there flame graphs or profiling capabilities?
Is the monitoring cost-effective (not oversampling)?
Is the infrastructure defined as code (Terraform, Pulumi)?
Is the cloud architecture well-documented?
Are there any single points of failure?
Is the infrastructure cost-optimized?
Is there a disaster recovery plan?
Is the network architecture secure (VPC, firewalls)?
Is the infrastructure reproducible (can be rebuilt from scratch)?
Is everything failing gracefully (not silently)?
Are there circuit breakers for external service calls?
Is there retry logic with exponential backoff?
Are there timeouts on all external calls?
Is the system designed for eventual consistency?
Are there health check endpoints?
Is the error handling following the Fallback Ban?
Is the local development setup documented and reproducible?
Can a new developer get started in < 30 minutes?
Is there a Makefile or equivalent for common tasks?
Are development dependencies version-locked?
Is the dev environment consistent with production?
Are there development-only features gated correctly?
Is the developer experience (DX) a priority?
Are dependencies scanned for known vulnerabilities?
Is SAST (static analysis) running in the pipeline?
Is DAST (dynamic analysis) running against staging?
Are secrets rotated on a schedule?
Is there a security incident response plan?
Are container images scanned before deployment?
Is the principle of least privilege followed?
Are there SLOs (Service Level Objectives) defined?
Is there an error budget tracked?
Is the uptime target realistic and measured?
Is there an on-call rotation (or at least monitoring)?
Is there a runbook for common incidents?
Is post-incident review (blameless) practiced?
Is the toil budget tracked and reduced?
Is the branching strategy documented (main, prod, feature branches)?
Are pre-commit hooks enforcing code quality?
Are pre-push hooks preventing broken builds?
Is the commit message format consistent?
Is there branch protection on main/prod?
Is the git history clean (no force pushes to shared branches)?
Are large files managed correctly (git-lfs or .gitignore)?
Are all tests deterministic (no flaky tests)?
Is test isolation maintained (no shared state between tests)?
Are tests running in parallel for speed?
Is test coverage measured and reported?
Are integration tests separated from unit tests?
Are there end-to-end tests for critical paths?
Is the test infrastructure itself tested?
Are Firestore indexes configured for all queries?
Is the data model documented?
Are there backup mechanisms for Firestore data?
Is read/write cost optimized (batch operations, pagination)?
Are there data migration scripts for schema changes?
Is the Firestore security rules file correct?
Are there monitoring alerts for Firestore costs?
Is structured logging implemented (JSON format)?
Are log levels used correctly (DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR)?
Are PII and secrets excluded from logs?
Is there centralized log aggregation?
Are logs searchable and filterable?
Is log retention configured appropriately?
Are there log-based alerts for critical errors?
Is the API versioned (/api/v1/)?
Is rate limiting applied at the API gateway level?
Is CORS configured correctly?
Are API responses cached where appropriate?
Is the API documentation auto-generated (Swagger/OpenAPI)?
Are API errors returning consistent error formats?
Is the API health check endpoint returning useful information?
Are all endpoints using HTTPS?
Is TLS 1.3 enforced (no downgrade)?
Are SSL certificates auto-renewed?
Is HSTS configured with appropriate max-age?
Are certificate pinning requirements documented?
Is the SSL configuration following Mozilla's recommended settings?
Are there monitoring alerts for certificate expiry?
Is DNS configured correctly for all domains?
Is there a CDN for static assets?
Is the TTL configured appropriately?
Are DNS changes version-controlled?
Is there DNS monitoring for availability?
Is there a CDN cache invalidation strategy?
Are subdomains configured correctly (api, app, docs)?
Is there a backup strategy for all persistent data?
Are backups tested regularly (restore drills)?
Is the RPO (Recovery Point Objective) defined?
Is the RTO (Recovery Time Objective) defined?
Are backups encrypted at rest?
Are backups stored in a separate region/account?
Is there automated backup verification?
Is the build system simple and fast?
Is the code compiled without warnings?
Are there unnecessary abstractions that should be removed?
Is the codebase navigable without IDE assistance?
Is the coding style consistent throughout?
Are there any files that do too many things?
Is the project structure logical and discoverable?
Are there any code smells that need refactoring?
Is the architecture documented with diagrams?
Are there clear boundaries between modules?
Is there a strangler fig pattern for legacy migration?
Are there any circular dependencies?
Is the test pyramid balanced (unit > integration > e2e)?
Are there feature toggles for gradual rollouts?
Are all dependencies pinned to specific versions?
Is there a lock file (requirements.txt, package-lock.json)?
Are there any known vulnerabilities in dependencies?
Is the dependency tree minimal (no unnecessary packages)?
Are there alternative dependencies that are better maintained?
Is there a process for updating dependencies?
Are there any deprecated dependencies still in use?
Are environment variables documented in .env.example?
Is there a clear separation between dev/staging/production configs?
Are secrets never committed to git?
Is there a secret management solution (GCP Secret Manager)?
Are feature flags environment-specific?
Is the configuration validated at startup?
Are there any hardcoded values that should be configurable?
Is the Fallback Ban enforced (no silent failures)?
Are error messages helpful and actionable?
Is there error tracking (Sentry, Cloud Error Reporting)?
Are errors categorized (user error vs system error)?
Are retry-able errors distinguished from permanent failures?
Is there a global error handler for unhandled exceptions?
Are error responses consistent across all endpoints?
Is there a migration framework for schema changes?
Are migrations reversible?
Are migrations tested before production deployment?
Is the migration history tracked?
Are zero-downtime migrations supported?
Is there a data migration strategy for major version upgrades?
Are migrations idempotent (safe to run multiple times)?
Is the system horizontally scalable?
Are there bottlenecks at specific load levels?
Is the database designed for scale (partitioning, caching)?
Is the API stateless (no session affinity required)?
Is there load testing infrastructure?
Is auto-scaling configured correctly?
Is the system tested at 10x current load?
Is there a budget alert configured in GCP?
Is the monthly cloud spend tracked?
Are there any unused resources still incurring costs?
Is the cost per user calculated and trending?
Are there cost optimization recommendations being followed?
Is committed use discount appropriate for the workload?
Is the cost breakdown documented (compute, storage, network)?
Is there a CONTRIBUTING.md for new developers?
Is the deployment process documented step by step?
Is the architecture documented with diagrams?
Are runbooks available for common operational tasks?
Is the troubleshooting guide up to date?
Are post-mortems documented and accessible?
Is the documentation version-controlled with the code?
Is the network segmented appropriately?
Are ingress/egress rules configured correctly?
Is there a WAF (Web Application Firewall) in place?
Are internal services not exposed to the internet?
Is there DDoS protection?
Is network traffic encrypted in transit?
Are there network monitoring and alerting rules?
Is there chaos testing to validate resilience?
What happens when the database is unavailable?
What happens when an external API times out?
Is the system tested for partial failures?
Is there a game day or disaster recovery drill schedule?
Are failure modes documented?
Is the blast radius of failures contained?
Are compliance checks automated in the pipeline?
Is there automated license compliance checking?
Is there automated security policy enforcement?
Are compliance artifacts generated automatically?
Is there audit trail for all changes?
Are compliance requirements documented as code?
Is there SOC2 readiness tracking?
Is there a unified CI/CD strategy across all 9 projects?
Are cross-project dependencies managed?
Is there a monorepo vs multi-repo strategy documented?
Are shared libraries versioned and published?
Is there a dependency graph across projects?
Are there integration tests that span multiple projects?
Is the deployment order documented for cross-project releases?
Is there a feature flag system for gradual rollouts?
Are feature flags cleaned up after full rollout?
Is there A/B testing infrastructure?
Are feature flags documented?
Is there a kill switch for any feature?
Are feature flags per-user or per-environment?
Is the feature flag system itself tested?
Is the API versioning strategy documented?
Are deprecated endpoints marked and scheduled for removal?
Is there backward compatibility for at least one major version?
Are API changes communicated to consumers?
Is there an API changelog?
Are breaking changes detected automatically?
Is there a deprecation policy?
Are build artifacts versioned and stored?
Is there a container registry for Docker images?
Are artifacts signed for integrity?
Is there artifact retention policy?
Are build artifacts reproducible?
Is there a promotion pipeline (dev → staging → prod)?
Are artifacts scanned before promotion?
Is the algorithm design documented for complex operations?
Are edge cases handled in critical code paths?
Is the code correct by construction (not by testing)?
Are mathematical operations verified for precision?
Is there formal verification for security-critical code?
Are invariants documented and enforced?
Is the code readable to a mathematician?
Are debugging tools and techniques documented?
Is there a standard way to reproduce issues?
Are error codes standardized?
Is there a bug triage process?
Are known issues tracked in a bug database?
Is there a 'fix it now' culture (no bug backlog)?
Are debugging sessions productive (systematic, not random)?
Is TDD practiced for new features?
Is the RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle followed?
Are tests written before code for critical paths?
Is the test suite a living specification?
Are tests expressing intent (not implementation)?
Is refactoring safe (tests catch regressions)?
Is the test suite fast enough to run on every save?
Is there an incident response process?
Are severity levels defined (P1-P4)?
Is there an escalation path documented?
Is there a communication plan for outages?
Are incidents tracked and reviewed?
Is there a post-incident review template?
Is the MTTR (Mean Time To Resolve) tracked?
Are accessibility tests automated in the CI pipeline?
Is there Lighthouse CI or similar running on every build?
Are WCAG violations blocking deployment?
Is there automated color contrast checking?
Is there screen reader testing in the pipeline?
Are accessibility regression tests in place?
Is the accessibility score tracked over time?
Is there an i18n build pipeline for locale files?
Are locale files validated in CI?
Is there automated translation quality checking?
Are missing translation keys caught before deployment?
Is the locale coverage tracked per language?
Is there a locale testing matrix?
Are RTL layouts tested automatically?
Are cryptographic operations using well-tested libraries?
Is key management following best practices?
Are random numbers generated from CSPRNG?
Is the vault encryption implementation reviewed?
Are hash functions used correctly (SHA-256 for integrity, PBKDF2 for keys)?
Is there key rotation capability?
Are timing attacks mitigated in auth code?
Are pre-commit hooks catching common issues?
Is the hook execution fast enough to not frustrate developers?
Are the hooks documented?
Can hooks be skipped in emergencies (with audit trail)?
Are the hooks version-controlled?
Are there pre-push hooks for expensive checks?
Is the hook failure message helpful and actionable?
Is there configuration drift detection between environments?
Are all configuration changes tracked?
Is the configuration validated against a schema?
Are there alerts for unexpected configuration changes?
Is the configuration documented?
Are default values sane and documented?
Is there a configuration change approval process?
Is there a release process documented?
Are releases tagged in git?
Is there a changelog generated for each release?
Are releases tested in staging before production?
Is there a release cadence (weekly, bi-weekly)?
Is there a hotfix process for critical issues?
Are release notes communicated to users?
Are all OSS licenses compatible with the project's license?
Is there a NOTICE file with required attributions?
Are there any copyleft dependencies in the proprietary code?
Is the FSL license correctly applied to solace-browser?
Are dependency licenses checked automatically?
Is there a policy for evaluating new dependencies?
Are there any license conflicts across the 9 projects?
Is there a 'you build it, you run it' culture?
Is the team capable of deploying independently?
Is there continuous learning from incidents?
Is automation prioritized over manual processes?
Is there a blameless culture for failures?
Is technical debt tracked and addressed?
Is the team's cognitive load manageable?
Is the DevOps maturity consistent across all 9 projects?
Are there shared DevOps patterns and tools?
Is there a platform team or shared infrastructure?
Are there cross-project deployment dependencies documented?
Is the total DevOps tooling cost justified?
Is there a unified monitoring dashboard?
Are there DevOps standards that all projects follow?
Do I create reproducible builds when I modify code?
Do I respect the deployment pipeline (not bypassing CI)?
Do I maintain test coverage when adding new code?
Do I follow the commit message conventions?
Do I check for security implications in my changes?
Do I update documentation when I change behavior?
Do I leave the codebase better than I found it?
Can the entire system be rebuilt from scratch using only git and documentation?
Is the system resilient to the departure of any single team member?
Is the deployment pipeline a competitive advantage?
Is the system secure enough for enterprise customers?
Is the operational cost sustainable at 10x the current scale?
Is the DevOps maturity level appropriate for the product stage?
Would a DevOps expert be proud to inherit this system?
